userStories.title: Write the hook and introduction
userStories.description: Write the opening section that hooks the reader and sets up the problem being solved. Should be 2-3 paragraphs max.
acceptanceCriteria: Opens with a concrete scenario or question
States the problem clearly within first 2 paragraphs
Hints at the solution without giving it all away
Under 200 words

userStories.title: Write the core technical explanation
userStories.description: The meat of the post. Explain the approach, include code examples, walk through the reasoning.
acceptanceCriteria: Includes at least one code snippet
Explains WHY not just WHAT
Each paragraph advances the narrative
Uses subheadings to break up sections

userStories.title: Write conclusion and takeaways
userStories.description: Wrap up with key learnings, what the reader should try next, and any caveats.
acceptanceCriteria: Summarizes the key insight in 1-2 sentences
Gives the reader a clear next action
Acknowledges limitations or trade-offs
Under 150 words

userStories.title: Revision pass: voice and flow
userStories.description: Read the full post end-to-end. Fix awkward transitions, repetitive words, passive voice, and filler phrases. Ensure it sounds like a human wrote it.
acceptanceCriteria: No paragraph starts with 'In this section' or similar meta-narration
No more than 2 sentences in a row start with 'The' or 'This'
All transitions between sections feel natural
Read time is under 8 minutes

userStories.title: Add metadata and frontmatter
userStories.description: Add the frontmatter block with title, description, tags, date, and any other fields required by the blog platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Title is compelling and under 65 characters
Meta description is under 160 characters
At least 3 relevant tags
Date is set correctly
